meta.title: Context overflow
meta.description: Asked to find the one thing to fix in a slow checkout, an agent reads its way through file after file until the window is full. To keep going it drops its oldest context — including the original request — then confidently answers a question no one asked. Token counts are hand-tuned estimates.

tools.name: read_file
tools.description: Open a file and see exactly what's inside.

events.summary: You are a coding agent investigating a performance problem. You can read files.

events.text: Customers say checkout is slow. Look through the code, find the cause, and tell me the single most important thing to fix first.
A performance question. The bottleneck could be anywhere in the checkout path, so I'll read checkout.js and each file it depends on before deciding.

events.output: // checkout.js — 214 lines. Orchestrates the checkout: builds the cart, prices it, reserves stock, records analytics, then redirects to payment. Imports cart-utils.js, pricing.js, inventory.js, analytics.js.
// cart-utils.js — 190 lines. Reads the cart from storage and normalizes line items. Re-parses the full cart on every call rather than caching it.
// pricing.js — 205 lines. Applies discounts and tax. Calls getCart() again internally and recomputes every promotion from scratch on each invocation.
// inventory.js — 198 lines. Reserves stock. Issues one database query per line item in a loop — a classic N+1 — instead of a single batched query.
events.annotation: The window is now 4042 / 4096 — almost full.
Full. The oldest context falls out to make room — the original request is in it.

events.text: Both pricing and cart-utils re-read the cart on every call, and inventory runs a query per line item. The interesting structural problem here is how much redundant work these modules do against the same data.

I've mapped how these three modules fit together. Pricing and cart-utils both re-read the cart on every call, and inventory issues one query per line item. The cleanest structural improvement would be to read the cart once and pass it through — want me to refactor them to share a single read?
